─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Technical Claims Reference ]─ ┐

Technical Claims Reference

Code-level evidence for every claim in the messaging kit. Each section maps a messaging claim to the specific code that backs it.

Session Acceleration

Claim: “Every session opens with full project context.”

Code: lib/banner.js:buildBanner() (lines 104-198) reads agent-rules, conventions (file or directory), active work (scanned frontmatter), registries, operator profile, knowledge map (ASCII trees), and local memory. Printed to stdout at SessionStart via hooks/session-init.js.

Limitation: The banner is injected text, not verified understanding. Context window pressure from large banners is unmeasured.

Skill and Fieldnote Persistence

Claim: “Snags become fieldnotes that fire forever.”

Code: Skills live in .lore/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with required frontmatter (name, description). scripts/sync-platform-skills.sh copies to .claude/skills/ and generates .cursor/rules/lore-*.mdc. The session banner includes a knowledge map tree showing all skills.

Limitation: Skills and fieldnotes are injected as available context, not as tool definitions. The agent must choose to reference them.

Worker Delegation

Claim: “Complex work delegates to focused workers.”

Code: lib/banner.js:getAgentNames() parses agent-registry.md for agent names. The session banner shows DELEGATION: Orchestrate, don't execute — delegate to: lore-worker-agent. hooks/prompt-preamble.js emits a per-prompt reminder. The orchestrator selects skills from skills-registry.md and spawns lore-worker-agent with task-specific context. Agents in .lore/agents/<name>.md carry per-platform model preferences resolved by sync-platform-skills.sh.

Limitation: Delegation is a text instruction to the LLM, not a routing mechanism. The agent chooses whether to delegate.

Convention Enforcement

Claim: “Conventions are enforced, not just documented.”

Code: hooks/convention-guard.js fires on PreToolUse for Write|Edit. Reads all .md files in docs/context/conventions/, extracts bold-formatted principle lines (**text**), injects security conventions on all writes, docs conventions for docs/ paths, work-item conventions for docs/workflow/, knowledge conventions for docs/knowledge/. Output is {"decision":"proceed","additional_context":"..."} — non-blocking but visible to the agent.

Limitation: Convention injection is context, not validation. The agent sees the rules but can still violate them.

Cross-Platform Sync

Claim: “One knowledge base, every platform, every repo.”

Code: scripts/sync-platform-skills.sh copies .lore/skills/ to .claude/skills/, resolves model cascades for .claude/agents/ (agent frontmatter -> .lore-config defaults -> omit), generates .cursor/rules/lore-*.mdc. lib/linked-rewrite.js rewrites paths for linked repos. LORE_HUB env var makes all banner/hook reads resolve from hub path.

Limitation: Sync is script-driven, not automatic. Users run capture/sync scripts (hooks prompt but don’t execute).

Zero Dependencies

Claim: “Zero runtime dependencies.”

Code: package.json has zero dependencies. All lib/ modules use Node.js built-ins only (fs, path, crypto, os, util, readline). create-lore also has zero npm dependencies.

Limitation: OpenCode adapter declares @opencode-ai/plugin as a dependency. Shell scripts require bash, awk, sed, node.

Setup Speed

Claim: “60-second setup, no configuration required.”

Code: create-lore/bin/create-lore.js does: validate name, shallow-clone at version tag, strip .git/, copy, write .lore-config, run git init. lib/sticky.js:ensureStickyFiles() scaffolds remaining templates on first session start.

Limitation: Requires Node 18+ and git. Shallow clone hits GitHub (network required at install time only).

Auditability

Claim: “Plain files, git-versioned, fully auditable.”

Code: All knowledge is markdown. All config is JSON. All hooks are readable JavaScript (50-100 lines each). All scripts are bash. No binary formats, no compiled artifacts, no opaque state. State files in .git/ (tracker counters) are ephemeral and not versioned.

MEMORY.md Protection

Claim: “Knowledge is routed to structured locations.”

Code: hooks/protect-memory.js fires on PreToolUse for Edit|Write|Read. Checks path.basename(filePath) === 'MEMORY.md' at project root. Returns {"decision":"block","reason":"..."} with routing instructions (skill → .lore/skills/, fieldnote → .lore/fieldnotes/, environment → docs/knowledge/, scratch → MEMORY.local.md). This is the only hard enforcement in the system.

Limitation: Bypassable via shell commands (the guard only fires on Edit/Write/Read tool types, not Bash).

Knowledge Tracker

Claim: “The agent is nudged to capture knowledge.”

Code: lib/tracker.js:processToolUse() classifies tool events. Maintains a consecutive-bash-command counter in .git/lore-tracker-{hash}.json. At threshold 3: nudge. At threshold 5: escalated warning. Counter resets on read-only tools or knowledge path writes. hooks/knowledge-tracker.js wires this to PostToolUse and PostToolUseFailure.

Limitation: Nudges are text in a context window. The LLM can ignore them. No enforcement at any threshold.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
